Specific Process Name Route: Acid-free dissolution of NdFeB magnet swarf using copper(II) nitrate; filtration to remove Cu/Fe precipitates; REE precipitation using ammonium hydroxide (50 °C, 1.5 h); oxalic acid to form RE oxalate; roasting at 800 °C to mixed REOs
Feedstock Focus: NdFeB manufacturing waste (magnet swarf from cutting/grinding)
Matrix Realism: True industrial matrix for feedstock assumptions (industrial-scale model at 100 metric tons swarf/year); solution chemistry derived from lab-scale dissolution/precipitation steps
Reported Nd Metrics: Overall REE extraction/recovery efficiency: 96%–99% (baseline scenario uses 97%) (TEA/LCA estimate); Baseline swarf composition assumed 27.19% Nd (TEA/LCA input)
Reported Dy Metrics: Dy handled as part of mixed REO output; baseline swarf composition assumed 0.48% Dy; scenarios include 0% Dy (96% leaching) and 6% Dy (99% leaching) (TEA/LCA inputs)
Reported Pr Metrics: Pr handled as part of mixed REO output; baseline swarf composition assumed 3.33% Pr (TEA/LCA input)
Selectivity Interference: Dissolution produces an REE-containing solution with copper and iron precipitates (qualitative; requires solids removal); wastewater treatment step described to remove iron with CaCl2 + Ca(OH)2 (qualitative)
Pretreatment Requirements: Filtration to remove insoluble residue/precipitates after dissolution; resultant solution pH ~4 noted; wastewater iron removal via CaCl2 + Ca(OH)2
Reagents Consumables: Copper(II) nitrate (primary dissolution reagent); ammonium hydroxide (precipitation); oxalic acid (oxalate formation); copper nitrate recycling efficiency ~70%; CaCl2 + Ca(OH)2 for iron removal
Energy Major Equipment: Scale-up example: 198-gallon reactor to process 100 tons of swarf/year; roasting energy cited as 16 kWh/kg of REO (4 kW muffle furnace; up to 4 h per 1 kg REO) (TEA/LCA estimate); filtration energy assumption 5.5 kWh/ton of dry material (TEA/LCA assumption)
Throughput Scale: Modeled industrial operation: 100 metric tons NdFeB magnet swarf/year → 32 metric tons REOs/year (TEA/LCA estimate)
Product Form Purity: Mixed REOs with ≥99.5% purity (not separated); REO output composition reported: 73%–89% Nd2O3; 9%–11% Pr2O3; 0%–18% Dy2O3
Capex: Not mentioned in source(s). (Capital costs are discussed as amortized over 20 years, but an explicit up-front CAPEX value is not extractable from provided text snippets.)
Opex Process: Total cost estimated $1,092,585–$1,525,596/year; also expressed as ~$11 per kg of swarf processed or ~$29–$36 per kg of mixed REO produced (TEA/LCA estimate)
Opex Waste Management Burden: Copper and iron precipitates require filtration (secondary solids stream); copper nitrate identified as major contributor to environmental burdens and recycling efficiency ~70% indicates ongoing losses/bleed (burden, not a disposal cost); waste-management cost line-items not mentioned in provided text snippets.
Evidence Level Trl Signal: TEA/LCA estimate based on lab-scale data with industrial-scale equipment sizing/modeling

Specific Process Name Route: Three counter-current stage solvent extraction using ~35 vol.% D2EHPA (in low-odor kerosene) at O/A 1.5; stripping with 1.25 M H2SO4 at 25 °C, A/O 0.5
Feedstock Focus: NdFeB manufacturing waste (coarsely ground NdFeB waste material <2 mm)
Waste Stream Type: Leachate: pregnant leach solution (PLS) from NdFeB waste dissolution
Matrix Realism: True industrial matrix: real PLS used (PLS composition reported for column feed); synthetic sulfate solutions also referenced for comparison
Reported Nd Metrics: Nd extraction: 99.9% Nd extracted with three counter-current stages (lab-scale); Nd stripping: >80% Nd stripped using 1.25 M H2SO4 at 25 °C, A/O 0.5 (lab-scale); PLS Nd concentration in column feed: 462 mg/L Nd (lab-scale feed)
Reported Dy Metrics: Dy extraction: >99.7% Dy (lab-scale); >99% Dy extraction at pH 1.9 (lab-scale)
Selectivity Interference: Co-extraction in three counter-current stages limited to 7% Fe, 4% B, 2% Co (lab-scale); Fe and Co stripping under 10% across conditions evaluated with 1.25 M H2SO4 (lab-scale)
Pretreatment Requirements: Upstream dissolution described as a patented cementation process; filtration step followed by addition of 0.1 vol.% H2SO4 to prevent oxidation of ferrous iron
Reagents Consumables: CuSO4·5H2O lixiviant preparation reported as 356 g in 1 L (lab-scale recipe); D2EHPA (97%) in low-odor kerosene (organic phase); pH control with 2 M NH3OH and 1 M H2SO4; oxalic acid (1 M) for scrubbing (>86% Fe and >88% B scrubbing)
Energy Major Equipment: Lab equipment: SX/stripping in 250 mL beaker; hotplate with magnetic stirring noted
Throughput Scale: Lab-scale batch SX: all SX/stripping tests in 250 mL glass beaker
Product Form Purity: Product form described as REE oxalate intermediates that can be calcined to RE oxides (no numeric purity for SX-only product)
Opex Waste Management Burden: Iron remaining in raffinate treated with neutralizing agents; iron-removal precipitation described as potentially slow and requiring control for filterability (qualitative waste-handling burden)
Evidence Level Trl Signal: lab-scale (bench SX/stripping performed in 250 mL beaker)

Specific Process Name Route: Reverse-phase extraction chromatography column impregnated with D2EHPA (C18-type), with H2SO4 gradient elution; Sm and Dy recovered last (required eluate concentrations: >0.4 M H2SO4 and >0.9 M H2SO4, respectively)
Feedstock Focus: NdFeB manufacturing waste (PLS derived from NdFeB waste)
Waste Stream Type: Leachate: pregnant leach solution (PLS) fed to column
Matrix Realism: Mixed: real PLS column feed; chromatographic separation performance discussed relative to a real leach solution (also uses synthetic solution in testing)
Reported Nd Metrics: Product purity: combined Nd+Pr purity >98% (lab-scale chromatography output); Sm control: Nd fraction targeted to <0.01% Sm (lab-scale target/criterion)
Reported Dy Metrics: Dy recovery noted as last fraction; Dy elution requires >0.9 M H2SO4 (lab-scale chromatography condition)
Reported Pr Metrics: Real PLS enabled recovery of >50% Pr in a third fraction with purity >75% (lab-scale); PLS Pr concentration in column feed: 38 mg/L (lab-scale feed)
Selectivity Interference: If large amounts of Fe impurities pass upstream operations, a reductive eluant (e.g., oxalic acid) may be required (qualitative interference management)
Pretreatment Requirements: PLS prepared by filtration and stabilized with 0.1 vol.% H2SO4 (to prevent ferrous iron oxidation) before downstream separation
Reagents Consumables: H2SO4 eluate for gradient separation (including >0.4 M and >0.9 M H2SO4 conditions); other specific consumables (e.g., ethanol impregnation solvent) not mentioned in provided text snippets.
Energy Major Equipment: Column temperature control using BioTek Instruments HPLC 582 thermostat
Throughput Scale: Lab-scale: 50 µL feed injections; constant eluate flowrate 1 mL/min (chromatography)
Product Form Purity: Separated fractions including Nd+Pr fraction (>98% combined purity) and a Pr-rich fraction (>75% purity) (lab-scale); REE oxalates can be calcined to oxides
Evidence Level Trl Signal: lab-scale chromatography (50 µL injections; 1 mL/min flow)

Technology Class: hydromet + solvent extraction (oxidation roasting + HCl leach + Aliquat 336 SX + oxalate precipitation) (older study, 2018)
Specific Process Name Route: Oxidation roasting (e.g., 600 °C, 6 h) to remove cooling reagent and reduce Fe dissolution; leaching with HCl (0.5 N); selective leaching removed 89% Fe (0.5 M HCl; S:L 1:100; 4 h; 95 °C); Fe solvent extraction using Aliquat 336 (0.1 M A336; 3 M chloride ion; A:O 1:3; mixed 1 min); Nd precipitation with oxalic acid; calcination of Nd oxalate at 900 °C (0.5 h) to Nd2O3
Feedstock Focus: NdFeB manufacturing waste (acoustic magnet swarf slurry, AMSS)
Waste Stream Type: Swarf/slurry with coolant: AMSS contains 52.5% cooling reagent
Matrix Realism: True industrial matrix (AMSS composition reported: 12.45% Nd; 34.35% Fe; 0.48% B; 52.5% cooling reagent)
Reported Nd Metrics: Nd dissolution: 99% of neodymium dissolved in 0.5 N HCl (lab-scale); Nd recovery: 99% (lab-scale); Oxalic acid demand: 18 g per L solution to precipitate 99% Nd (lab-scale); Final product purity: over 99% (Nd2O3) (lab-scale)
Selectivity Interference: Phosphorous-based extractants not suitable due to co-extraction of iron and REEs (qualitative); Co-precipitation causes 20%–30% metal loss in leachate (lab-scale); Selective leaching removed 89% Fe under stated HCl conditions (lab-scale)
Pretreatment Requirements: Drying by furnace (moisture content reported 50%); oxidation roasting removes cooling reagent and decreases iron leaching percent
Reagents Consumables: HCl (0.5 N) for leaching; Aliquat 336 extractant (0.1 M A336 used for extraction) and 3 M chloride ion condition reported; oxalic acid: 18 g/L to precipitate 99% Nd
Energy Major Equipment: Oxidation roasting at 600 °C for 6 h; calcination at 900 °C (0.5 h) to obtain neodymium oxide; leaching at 95 °C for 4 h
Throughput Scale: Lab-scale experiments; context note: AMSS production stated as 1900 tons/year worldwide (market context, not plant throughput)
Product Form Purity: Neodymium oxide (Nd2O3) reported as 99% purity after calcination at 900 °C for 0.5 h
Opex Waste Management Burden: Cooling reagent comprises 52.5% of AMSS and is important to remove before leaching (implies significant organic/coolant handling); co-precipitation causes 20%–30% metal loss to solids (waste burden)

Technology Class: hydromet + solvent extraction (hybrid oxidative roasting + selective leaching + oxalate precipitation)
Specific Process Name Route: Oxidative roasting at 550–800 °C; selective leaching using H2SO4 at 2.5 M over 24 h; drying and roasting at 700–800 °C; precipitation by oxalic acid; calcination to obtain Nd2O3
Waste Stream Type: Sintering scrap/fragments (sintered NdFeB waste ground to under 70 mesh)
Matrix Realism: True industrial matrix (waste composition reported: 23.6% Nd; 60.3% Fe; 0.261% B)
Reported Nd Metrics: Feed composition reported: 23.6% Nd (no extractable Nd recovery %, residual concentration, or capacity in provided text snippets)
Selectivity Interference: High Fe content in feed (60.3% Fe) noted; no quantitative selectivity coefficients or interference tests reported in provided text snippets
Pretreatment Requirements: Grinding to under 70 mesh prior to oxidative roasting
Reagents Consumables: H2SO4 (2.5 M) for 24 h leaching; oxalic acid for precipitation
Energy Major Equipment: High-temperature roasting steps at 550–800 °C and 700–800 °C (furnace/roaster implied; energy intensity not reported)
Product Form Purity: Neodymium oxide (Nd2O3) described as “high purity” but no purity (%) reported in provided text snippets
Opex Waste Management Burden: Iron sulfate and neodymium sulfate mainly obtained; iron sulfate converted to hematite during roasting step (implies solids handling)
Evidence Level Trl Signal: lab-scale (research study on manufacturing waste; no pilot/commercial indicators in provided snippets)

Specific Process Name Route: Flash reduction in a flash furnace (H2/N2 reducing gas) followed by two-step wet magnetic separation
Feedstock Focus: NdFeB manufacturing waste (scraps from cutting, grinding, polishing; provided by manufacturer)
Matrix Realism: True industrial matrix (scraps provided by Ganzhou YouLi Technology; batch experiments on 100 g)
Reported Nd Metrics: Not mentioned in source(s). (Reported as total REOs, not element-specific.)
Selectivity Interference: Feed/calcine composition reported (e.g., 65.5% FeOx; 30.2% REOs; 0.5% CoO; 0.1% CuO, etc.) and authors state the process minimizes Fe interference in subsequent leaching (qualitative)
Pretreatment Requirements: Pretreatment steps listed: roasting, degreasing, grinding, oxidizing roasting; high-energy ball milling with 20:1 ball/materials ratio
Reagents Consumables: Reducing gas: mixed H2 (30%) and N2 (70%); reported flow rates: H2 2 L/min and N2 0.3 L/min (experimental conditions)
